channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court for permission to resume deporting Venezuelan migrants under wartime law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Friday asked the Supreme Court for permission to resume deportations of Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador under an 18th century wartime law, while a court fight continues.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump endorses budget fix that would restore Washington, DC, shortfall and urges House to pass it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is pushing the House of Representatives to pass a measure that would prevent immediate financial disaster for the District of Columbia even as he continues to level harsh criticism at the city and its leaders.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Severe storms flood streets and prompt water rescues in South Texas
item.description: ALAMO, Texas (AP) — South Texas worked to dry out on Friday after severe thunderstorms a day earlier flooded streets, forced the closure of an airport and compelled dozens of water rescues as many drivers were forced to abandon their vehicles on roads and parking lots as some areas were inundated wi
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Social media influencer promotes clean eating amid federal push to ban food dyes
item.description: As efforts to ban the use of synthetic dyes in food gain ground in several states across the United States, a social media influencer is using her platform to help people become more aware of the harmful ingredients in their food.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, Fox 5

item.title: Top-rated seafood restaurant in every state: Report
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Putin suggests putting Ukraine under UN-sponsored external governance
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin proposed Friday to temporarily put Ukraine under external governance as part of efforts to reach a peaceful settlement, in remarks that reflected the Kremlin leader's determination to achieve his war goals.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Musk deletes post promising he'd go to Wisconsin to deliver $2 million to Supreme Court race voters
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — Billionaire Elon Musk on Friday deleted a social media post in which he had announced plans to hold a rally in Wisconsin to “personally hand over” $2 million to a pair of voters who have already cast their ballots in the state’s hotly contested Supreme Court race.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to ban fluoride in public drinking water
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — Utah has become the first state to ban fluoride in public drinking water, over opposition from dentists and national health organizations who warn the move will lead to medical problems and disproportionately affect low-income communities. Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW BROWN and HANNAH SCHOENBAUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: This is the top-rated seafood restaurant in California, according to Yelp
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner. Knowing this, the analysts at Yelp have sought to identify the top-rated seafood restaurants in each state.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) warned in a speech Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline if they agree to a deal that achieves only “illusory peace.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: A mayor's lawsuit shut down a newspaper over fake stories. Now he faces corruption allegations
item.description: EVERETT, Mass. (AP) — For years, the mayor of a Boston suburb dreaded Wednesdays. That was the day when a local weekly would publish shocking allegations that he was on the take, sexually harassing women or under investigation by the FBI.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Myanmar at a glance: Embroiled in civil war, now facing more devastation after powerful earthquake
item.description: Even before a powerful 7.7-magnitude quake hit Myanmar on Friday, more than 3 million people in the country had been displaced, and hundreds of thousands were cut off from vital food and health programs as a result of a destructive four-year civil war that international groups claim has indiscrimina
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A second South Carolina death row inmate chooses execution by firing squad
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina death row inmate on Friday chose execution by firing squad, just five weeks after the state carried out its first death by bullets. Mikal Mahdi, who pleaded guilty to murder for killing a police officer in 2004, is scheduled to be executed April 11.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate Republican chair challenges Trump over emergency funding move
item.description: Sen. Susan Collins (R-Maine), the head of the powerful Senate Appropriations Committee, and her Democratic counterpart are challenging the Trump administration’s handling of emergency funding included in legislation passed earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump holds 'productive call' with new Canadian prime minister
item.description: President Trump spoke with Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Friday for the first time since Carney was elected earlier in the month amid tensions between the two nations.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: DOJ asks Supreme Court to intervene in deportation flights case
item.description: The Trump administration asked the Supreme Court on Friday to allow it to invoke the Alien Enemies Act to carry out swift deportations.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawyer for Michigan couple held in Mexican prison says resort company is at fault
item.description: A Michigan couple remains behind bars in a Mexican prison, accused of breaching a timeshare contract — but their lawyer said the resort that filed the criminal complaint against them breached the contract first.
item.dc:creator: Shajaka Shelton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DOJ investigating California universities over affirmative action
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) announced Thursday it will be investigating multiple California universities to ensure they are in compliance with a Supreme Court ruling banning affirmative action in admissions.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Americans’ outlook on economy becomes a bit more pessimistic: Poll
item.description: Around 57 percent of those earning under $50,000 said their financial situation is “bad,” while 39 percent argued it is “good,” the poll found.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Georgia mom’s Walmart trip devolves into ‘tug-of-warring’ in desperate attempt to save her son
item.description: A Georgia mother is in shock and a man is behind bars after he allegedly tried to grab a 2-year-old child while the family was shopping in a local Walmart.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 150
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Lights, Camera, Relocation: Utahns react as Sundance films its final scene in Utah
item.description: Park City businesses are reacting to Sundance's decision to move to another location, with residents confident that the city will continue to be a world-class destination for arts and entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Renisha Mall, ABC 4

item.title: Trump order targets 'divisive narratives' in Smithsonian museums
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday signed an executive order seeking to exert more control over what is displayed at Smithsonian museums and federal sites, citing what the White House called “divisive narratives” promoted by certain museums.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Vandal strikes Cybertruck, Tesla owner hits back
item.description: An accused vandal is "under investigation" after appearing to drag a key across a Tesla Cybertruck outside of the Omni Mountain Resort & Spa in Brenton Woods, New Hampshire.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: N.C. Green Beret planned to divorce his wife before disappearance
item.description: Clinton Bonnell's girlfriend said he texted her the night before he went missing, saying he told his wife he was filing for divorce, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Justin Moore, NewsNation

item.title: Ivy League anti-Israel ringleader Mahmoud Khalil fights deportation in New Jersey court
item.description: The Trump administration has sought to deport Columbia University graduate Mahmoud Khalil for leading the pro-Palestinian protests and sit-ins at the Ivy League institution.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian scientist at Harvard Medical School detained by ICE at Boston airport
item.description: A Russian scientist working at Harvard Medical School was detained by ICE at Boston Logan International Airport when attempting to bring back frog embryos at the request of a professor.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: First rain and then fire chase people from their homes in North and South Carolina
item.description: When Nicole Taylor and her family moved to their new home in the South Carolina mountains six months ago, the gorgeous view of Table Rock Mountain was the clincher.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Battle over proxy voting for new parents intensifies in House GOP
item.description: The battle between Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) and House GOP leaders over looming floor action to allow proxy voting for new parents is intensifying as both sides of the heated debate dig in their heels — with the clash potentially coming to a head as soon as next week.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Judge says heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional as states face mounting lawsuits
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — A federal judge's rebuke of Texas housing prisoners in lockups without air conditioning stopped short of ordering a fix before summer in what has become of the country's biggest lawsuits over keeping prisoners safe during dangerous extreme heat. But U.S.
item.dc:creator: JIM VERTUNO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Philadelphia detectives in perjury trial involving a 2016 murder exoneration
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The trial of three long-retired Philadelphia detectives accused of lying about evidence in a homicide case that later ended with an exoneration wrapped up with a mixed verdict Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Health and Human Services will lay off 10,000 workers in major restructuring
item.description: In a major overhaul, the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services will lay off 10,000 workers and shut down entire agencies, including ones that oversee billions of dollars in funds for addiction services and community health centers across the country.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Seitz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man arrested in Molotov cocktail attack at Tesla shop in Las Vegas will also face federal charges
item.description: Las Vegas police on Thursday said a man suspected of attacking a Tesla location with Molotov cocktails and damaging at least five vehicles has been arrested.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Violin thief-turned-bank robber sentenced to 4 years in prison
item.description: A California man convicted of orchestrating a scheme to steal valuable violins, and then robbing a bank when he realized federal investigators were closing in on him, has been sentenced.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: NY county clerk refuses to file Texas' fine for doctor accused of prescribing abortion pills
item.description: A county clerk in New York refused Thursday to file a more than $100,000 judgment from Texas against a doctor accused of prescribing abortion pills to a woman near Dallas, setting up a potential challenge to laws designed to shield abortion providers who serve patients in states with abortion bans.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House withdrawing Stefanik nomination to serve as US ambassador to UN
item.description: The White House has informed the chair of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee that it is withdrawing Rep. Elise Stefanik’s (R-N.Y.) nomination to serve as U.S. ambassador to the United Nations, reflecting how critical her vote is in the House to passing President Trump’s agenda.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: House Democrats demand answers from Trump team about war plans Signal chat
item.description: The top Democrats on the House’s Defense, Oversight, Foreign Affairs, and Intelligence committees want answers from President Trump’s national security team, saying they’ve become “deeply troubled” after a journalist was unknowingly added to a group chat of senior officials discussing war plans.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, WGN 9
